per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA describes website data collection and tracking, while the REG requires legislative measures to contain provisions; they do not express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

The REA states that websites may collect data and use tracking, while the REG concerns legislative measures containing provisions about categories of personal data; no quote-supported direct mismatch is expressed.

The REA concerns possible website tracking activities, whereas the REG concerns legislative provisions about risks to rights and freedoms; they are not the same constraint and no direct conflict is stated.
